description: Findings impact propagation map — which SOT files/sections are affected by which findings

ripples.propagation: Schema field rename would require PART2 update + all code referencing LogEvent fields
ripples.resolution: Determine canonical field names (SOT D2.1-D2 wins), update PART2 references

ripples.propagation: Timeout value affects SDAR escalation timing, approval workflow UX, and config defaults
ripples.resolution: Resolve MASTER_SPEC vs CLAUDE.md — likely MASTER_SPEC (30min) is authoritative per RULE 1.3 hierarchy

ripples.propagation: Opus model change affects cost calculations and capability assumptions for V2
ripples.resolution: Add V2 model upgrade note to PART2 §6.7

ripples.propagation: RAG limits affect chunk retrieval performance and context window management
ripples.resolution: Add RAG operation limits to PART2 §6.5 implementation notes

ripples.propagation: Python 3.12 vs 3.11+ affects dependency compatibility across all modules
ripples.resolution: Resolve to 3.11+ (VAMOS-wide) — AI_INVESTING_SPEC should align or document exception

ripples.propagation: Missing schemas in V0 table could cause implementation gaps
ripples.resolution: Verify V0 scope — Phase-A schemas may be V1+ items

ripples.propagation: Lifecycle states affect state machine implementation and testing
ripples.resolution: Add LOCK lifecycle reference to PART2 §6.7

ripples.propagation: Interface contract needed for adapter pattern implementation
ripples.resolution: Add VectorStore adapter interface summary to PART2 §6.5

cross_cutting_patterns: Most findings (67%) are P3_omission — PART2 intentionally delegates details to SOT. These are documentation completeness issues, not errors.
LOCK violation count = 0 — all LOCK values are consistent (major improvement from v6).
Numeric mismatches (P1) dropped from v6 — Phase 0 SOT fixes resolved major value conflicts.
Schema-related findings cluster in Agent 5 — D2.1 schema documents are most divergent from PART2.
